commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR makes explicit subagent `runTimeoutSeconds` produce terminal timeout outcomes, adjusts timeout announce cleanup for delete mode, and adds focused registry/announce regressions.

PR surface: Source +33, Tests +125. Total +158 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main retries plain `agent.wait` timeouts without terminal snapshot fields, and the PR’s remaining defect is source-reproducible because the hard-deadline check runs after the full wait begins. I did not run a live current-main repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can keep the PR’s timeout behavior while moving the deadline check before waits or reducing waits to the remaining deadline.

target.title: fix(agents): enforce subagent run timeouts
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `sessions_spawn` accepted `runTimeoutSeconds`, but the subagent registry could keep a child run active when `agent.wait` returned a plain timeout without terminal snapshot fields.
- Why this matters now: #87444 reports parent sessions parked behind timed-out children, which can block completion delivery and consume active subagent capacity.
- Intended outcome: explicit subagent run timeouts now produce a terminal timeout outcome at the stored deadline; keep-mode parent wake can proceed while the child provider abort is still unwinding; delete-mode cleanup stays retryable while the active child cannot yet be removed.
- Intentionally out of scope: manual kill/reap behavior is left to #87445; this PR only handles the configured run timeout deadline.
- Success looks like: a child remains active before its deadline, then records `status: "timeout"` at the deadline, no active child run remains for the keep-mode proof, and the parent continuation receives the timeout completion event.
- Reviewer focus: `src/agents/subagent-registry-run-manager.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-announce.ts`, and the focused regressions in `src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts` / `src/agents/subagent-announce.timeout.test.ts`.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #87444

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #87445

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Requested through the maintainer workflow for #87444.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` child runs with explicit `runTimeoutSeconds` no longer remain active after the deadline when `agent.wait` only reports a non-terminal timeout; the timeout completion wakes the parent path instead of waiting for the child provider abort to finish.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source worktree running a real gateway/server and real `agent` RPC against a loopback OpenAI Responses-compatible provider harness. The parent model turn called `sessions_spawn`, then `sessions_yield`; the child provider request was held open past `runTimeoutSeconds: 1`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-issue-87444-proof.mts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce.timeout.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`; `git diff --check`. After the cleanup-retry amendment, reran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-announce.timeout.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts` and `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix: copied terminal output from the real gateway proof run:

```text
ISSUE_87444_PARENT_WOKE_C7A617
ISSUE_87444_REAL_BEHAVIOR_PROOF pass
{
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:issue-87444-c7a617",
  "taskName": "proof_child_timeout",
  "runId": "6298edb3-70e5-4926-91f3-9e98ebdb4ed1",
  "runTimeoutSeconds": 1,
  "outcomeStatus": "timeout",
  "elapsedMs": 1000,
  "deadlineDeltaMs": 0,
  "activeChildrenAfterTimeout": 0,
  "parentWakeDispatchCompleted": true,
  "providerRequestLabels": [
    "parent:function_call:sessions_spawn",
    "parent:function_call:sessions_yield",
    "child:provider_request_held_open",
    "parent:message:timeout_observed"
  ],
  "childProviderLateResponseServedBeforeParentWake": false
}
ISSUE_87444_PROOF_REQUEST_LOG ["parent:function_call:sessions_spawn","parent:function_call:sessions_yield","child:provider_request_held_open","parent:message:timeout_observed"]
```

Observed result after fix: the real gateway run accepted `sessions_spawn` with `runTimeoutSeconds: 1`, the held-open child recorded `outcomeStatus: "timeout"` at exactly `elapsedMs: 1000`, `activeChildrenAfterTimeout` was `0`, and the parent continuation produced the redacted wake token `ISSUE_87444_PARENT_WOKE_C7A617`.

What was not tested: external hosted provider credentials, external channel delivery, GitHub Actions CI completion, and the separate manual kill/reap path tracked by #87445.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: the provider endpoint was a loopback deterministic OpenAI Responses-compatible harness so no API keys or external messages were used; the exercised OpenClaw path was still the real gateway, real agent RPC, real `sessions_spawn`, real `sessions_yield`, registry timeout completion, and parent wake dispatch.

Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before the final fix, the same real gateway proof either left the timeout completion waiting behind child-provider abort/no-output handling or repeatedly spawned another child instead of producing the parent wake token; before the original registry change, the focused regression kept `completedRun?.endedAt` undefined after a plain `agent.wait` timeout.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node --import tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-issue-87444-proof.mts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce.timeout.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added registry coverage for non-terminal `agent.wait` timeouts at and near the stored explicit run deadline, plus announce coverage proving `cleanup: "delete"` timeout cleanup remains retryable while the embedded child request is still active.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The new registry regression failed before the production change because the child run stayed active and `endedAt` remained `undefined` after the explicit timeout deadline. ClawSweeper also found that the previous announce branch could return `didAnnounce === true` for `cleanup: "delete"` while deletion was skipped, marking cleanup complete with no retry.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A; focused regression tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Prematurely marking a still-running child as timed out if the gateway wait path returns a plain timeout before the configured child deadline, or finalizing delete cleanup while the child is still active.

How is that risk mitigated?

The registry branch only completes plain wait timeouts when `runTimeoutSeconds` is finite/positive and the current time is at the stored run deadline boundary. Existing session-store reconciliation still wins first, retry behavior remains unchanged before the deadline, and focused tests cover pre-deadline retry, boundary timeout completion, and delete-cleanup retryability while the embedded child is active.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Wait for GitHub CI and ClawSweeper re-review on the updated head/body.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

GitHub CI has not completed on the latest forced-pushed head; external hosted provider/channel proof was not run.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed ClawSweeper’s real-behavior proof request and the cleanup-delete finding: `cleanup: "delete"` timeout announce now returns `false` while the embedded child is still active, leaving cleanup retryable instead of marking deletion complete.
